Obviously, you must be a customer of both SAP Analytics Cloud planning and SAP Datasphere. Your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant must run on HANA Cloud (see above).

Is there a way to participate in the Controlled Release if my SAP Analytics Cloud tenant is not running on HANA Cloud?

The only way to participate if your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant is not running on HANA Cloud is the purchase of a new SAP Analytics Cloud tenant. However, the deployment of any new tenant will be aligned with your existing tenants. So, it must be ensured that it will be deployed on HANA Cloud instead. To do so, you can use a BTPEA agreement or you work with your account team to flag the delivery of the tenant as an exception with our operations teams.

Be aware that moving content built on new tenants to your old tenants once these are migrated is challenging. This is due to the 1:1 relationship between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere, the redundancy of objects deployed in different ways in the tenants and built content in SAP Datasphere. This is why the approach of a new SAP Analytics Cloud tenant on HANA Cloud for the Controlled Release is recommended for POC purposes only.

How can I sign up?

A registration page is available on Customer Influence. Link

When can I expect general availability?

General availability is planned for QRC1.2025. Note that availability only applies to SAP Analytics Cloud tenants running on HANA Cloud. Refer to this blogpost for more info on migration plans.

 

Conclusion

The upcoming controlled release of seamless planning marks a milestone in the journey to true extended planning & analysis (xP&A). Plan data now lives right next to actual data. Extend the capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud using SAP Datasphere’s capabilities. In the future, you shall exchange data across planning domains seamlessly. You shall see modeling and data management efforts become streamlined and efficient.

We are looking forward to welcoming many customers to this controlled release and to making seamless planning available across our customer base in 2025. We shall deliver additional functionality unlocking more of the potential of this incredibly powerful set-up.

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: Using the new selective deletion functionality in SAP DSP to track record deletion in the source
entry.content:
With the latest update of SAP Datasphere, it is now possible to create selective deletions on your local tables created in SAP Datasphere. Obviously, this opens a whole new world of possibilities. One of them is to be able to handle deletions in the source, and even track them in your SAP Datasphere environment. In this series of blogs, I will explain how you could set up such a scenario. In this first blog I will explain how the new functionality for selective deletions on local tables in SAP Datasphere is used.

The new functionality to do selective deletions on your local tables is available from the Data Integration Monitor. From the Local Tables we are able to jump into the settings for a specific local table in SAP Datasphere:

We now get into this new screen:

We are quite easily able to either delete all the records, or selectively delete records from a table based on the value in a column. It is also possible to do this on the values of multiple columns, instead of just one, to really make the deletion specific.

When using the Filter option we have quite a few options to make the correct selection of data that has to be deleted from the local table in SAP Datasphere:

When we have selected the value that we want the deletion to happen on, we can click the Show Preview option to actually show what will be deleted.

This pops up a preview of the data that will be deleted. I expected that this would just be a subset of records, but SAP Datasphere is actually giving a preview of the data that will be deleted, which gives a nice confirmation of what is going to happen.

When clicking on the refresh button in the previous screen we get another confirmation of the number of records that is going to be deleted and the size of the records that will be deleted. With this you could get a nice estimate of the results of your action, and if you need to broaden or narrow your selection to free up more or less space.

Now when we press the button SAP Datasphere will start the deletion of our data based on the criteria.

In the Data Deletion Logs tab, we can keep track of the deletions that previously happened on this table.

We are also able to jump into more detail in what the deletion was by clicking the arrow in window button on the right. Unfortunately, at this moment in time it is not yet possible to see what the delete statement was based on, it will just show you the amount of records deleted.

 

Creating a Data Deletion Schedule

In the same way as manually deleting data you can also set-up a schedule to selectively delete data. We can create a schedule by following the wizard. The first step is to define a business name for the deletion schedule and give the filter values to delete on:

 

The second step is to define a schedule in the same way that we can define a schedule for data loading.

 

Finally in the last step we get an overview of what we scheduled. 

Unfortunately, there is no clear overview over the next deletion that will take place, which I think is something that would add value to have. We can however differentiate the scheduled deletions from the manual deletions because the object type is different:

Another thing that I really miss at this moment in time is the possibility to schedule the selective deletion in a Task Chain. For the use case that we are going to build in the next blog this would be awesome to have.

Conclusion

The new selective deletion functionality in SAP Datasphere provides a powerful way to manage data within local tables. It allows users to selectively remove records based on specific criteria, offering a preview feature to verify what will be deleted. While there are some limitations—such as the lack of a clear overview of scheduled deletions and the inability to add deletions to a Task Chain—this functionality still offers significant control and flexibility.

Particularly for scenarios where data is loaded from external sources like flat files with "full updates," this feature helps manage deletions effectively. By combining it with the power of joins, users can track new, existing, and deleted records, maintaining a clean and up-to-date data environment. Stay tuned for the next blog post, which will explore a practical example of how to leverage this new capability to its fullest potential.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere - How to flip sign the Measure value by GL Account type like SAP Analytics Cloud.
entry.content:
Before we start discussing, I would like to let you know that you can get a 30-day trial instance of SAP Datasphere and access basic SAP Datasphere trainings here.  https://developers.sap.com/tutorial-navigator.html

As an SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) consultant, I am currently working for a customer who wants to use SAP Datasphere as a reporting data source for SAC. This will allow them to integrate SAC Planning data, Group Reporting Consolidated Actual data, and third-party data.

This is a common scenario, but the main problem is customer cannot figure out how to flip sign based on the GL Account type in SAP Datasphere. Additionally, GL Accounts usually have a hierarchical structure like Balance Sheet (B/S) and Profit & Loss Statement (P/L). Therefore, after the value is aggregated, it should be displayed with the correct sign.

In financial systems, Income and Liability accounts data are usually stored in the database with opposite signs, while Expense and Asset accounts data are stored as they are.

For example, if Revenue is 1000 and Expense is 300, the Net Revenue will be 700.

To achieve this, Revenue is stored as -1000 and Expense as 300. When added to together, the result is -700. Since the Net Revenue account is an Income Account, the Financial system will flip its sign before displaying it to users, showing Net Revenue as 700. 

This behavior is consistent across all SAP systems (ERP, BW, BPC and SAC.) 

Unfortunately, DataSphere does not yet provide this feature. I believe the SAP Datasphere development team is considering it, but we need a solution for financial reporting in the meantime.

The solution is....

'Create a calculated measure in the Analytic Model using a CASE WHEN Statement'.

Let's Assume, we have an attribute in the ACCT dimension called Actype, which has values 'INC', 'EXP,' AST and 'LEQ'. The measure name is Amount.

By creating a calculated measure called 'CM SIGNDATA' as shown below, we can display the correct sign data. The Amount measure shows the same sign as it is stored in the database.

CASE
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'INC’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    WHEN Actype_ACCT = 'LEQ’ THEN Amount * (-1)
    ELSE
          Amount
END

I hope SAP Datasphere supports this as built-in feature like SAC so that we don't need to create additional calculated Measure.

NOTE:  you can find Actype_ACCT attribute when you create the calculated measure. After selecting 'Dimension' and searching 'ACCT', you will find it. just click it then it will be inserted in the formula without typing.

I hope this article helps anyone facing similar issues.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: Exporting Tables from Datasphere to Hana Datalake File System using Replication Flow
entry.content:
Exporting Tables from DSP to HDLFS using Replication Flow

Introduction

The below is the process of exporting tables from SAP Datasphere (DSP) to SAP HANA Datalake Filesystem (HDLFS) in PARQUET file format using SAP Datasphere's Replication Flow (RF) feature. SAP DSP is a powerful platform designed to manage, model, and share data across enterprise systems. By integrating with SAP HDLFS, it enables organizations to store large volumes of structured and unstructured data efficiently for advanced analytics, archiving, or cost-effective data storage. The RF within SAP DSP simplifies data movement between different systems and environments by allowing seamless data replication.

Connection Creation

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Connections
    Go to the Connections tab: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Connections’ tab located on the left-hand side.

  • Create a New Connection
    Click on ‘Create’: In the Connections tab, click on the Add Connection button.

  • Choose Connection Type
    Choose SAP HANA Datalake: From the list of available connection types, select ‘SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Files’.

  • Configure Connection Properties
    Enter Connection Details: Fill in the required fields such as Host (Hostname of the SAP HDL instance) and Root Path (Path of the HDLFS where the table data needs to be stored) under Connection Details, Keystore File (.p12 format) and Keystore password under Credentials.

  • Add Connection Information and Save
    Type in Connection Details: Provide an appropriate Business Name for the connection. The Technical Name will be filled automatically upon filling Business Name and can be changed if required. Once the details are filled, Click on ‘Create Connections’ to finalize the connection setup. Note that the Business Name, Technical Name: DSP_TO_HDL is example. Replace them with the required names.

  • Test the Connection
    Click on ‘Validate Connection’: Upon creation of connection, it will visible in the 'Connections' tab. Ensure that the connection details are correct and click on the ‘Validate Connection’ button to verify the connection. The successful connection test gives a message 'Connection "DSP_TO_HDL" is valid'.

Replication Flow Creation

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Data Builder
    Go to the Data Builder tab: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Data Builder’ tab located on the left-hand side.

  • Create a New Replication Flow
    Click on ‘New Replication Flow’: In the Data Builder tab, click on the ‘New Replication Flow’ button.

  • Select Source Systems and Table
    Choose Source System: Click on 'Select Source Connection' and select ‘SAP Datasphere’ as the source system. Post this, click on 'Add Source Objects' to add the Repository Objects present in the SAP DSP. Select the required table and click on 'OK'.

Note that the Repository Object: LARGE_DATA is example. Select the required table.

  • Select Target Systems
    Choose Target System: Click on the 'Select Target Connection' and Select newly created ‘DSP_TO_HDL’ as the target system. Post this, click on 'Select Container' to select the path present in the SAP HDLFS. Select 'Root Path' (Home Button) or the required directory inside the Root Path and click on 'Select'.

  • Configure Settings
    Choose required settings: Click the 'Settings'. Here, the Load Type and Truncate can be selected. Load Type can be either Initial Only or Initial and Delta.
    • Initial Only: Load all selected data once
    • Initial and Delta: After the initial load, the system checks for source data changes (delta) at regular intervals and copies the changes to the target. The default value for the delta load interval is 60 minutes. You can change it in the side panel by entering an integer between 0 and 24 for hours and 0 and 59 for minutes, respectively. The maximum allowed value is 24 hours 0 minutes. If you enter 0 hours and 0 minutes, the system replicates any source changes immediately. This can be selected only if Delta Capture is turned on for a particular table.
    • Truncate: If Truncate is marked for a database table, when you start the replication run, the system deletes the table content, but leaves the table structure intact and fills it with the relevant data from the source. If not, the system inserts new data records after the existing data in the target. For data records that already exist in the target and have been changed in the source, the system updates the target records with the changed data from the source using the UPSERT mode.

  • Deploy Replication Flow
    Click on 'Deploy': Select the 'Deploy' button to save and deploy the Replication Flow. Provide an appropriate Business Name for the Flow. The Technical Name will be filled automatically upon filling Business Name and can be changed if required. Once the details are filled, click on ‘Save’ to finalize the Flow Deployment. Upon Successful Deployment, a message appears for the same and Deployed Status can be seen in the General section of Replication Flow Properties.

Note that the Business Name, Technical Name: LARGE_REP_4 is example. Replace them with the required names.

Executing Replication Flow

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Data Builder and access Replication Flow
    Go to the Data Builder tab and select Replication Flow: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Data Builder’ tab located on the left-hand side and click the newly created Replication Flow.

  • Run Replication Flow
    Click on 'Run': On the Replication Flow page, click the 'Run' button to start the execution of the replication flow. The status will be displayed in the Run Status section. To monitor the flow, click on 'Open in Flows Monitor' which navigates to Data Integration Monitor.

  • Replication Flow Completion
    Completion of Flow: Upon successful completion of the Replication Flow, a message 'DIRECT run of REPLICATION_FLOWS/RUN for LARGE_REP_4 is completed' will be displayed. The status can be seen both in the Run Status section of Replication Flow page as well the Data Integration Monitor.

  • Scheduling the Replication Flows
    Create Schedule: To frequently execute the Replication Flow, select the 'Schedule' button and click on 'Create Schedule' in the Data Integration Monitor. A pop-up window will appear consisting of Frequency and Time Range sections. Fill in the required details and click on 'Create'. The flow will then run according to the specified schedule.

PARQUET Files in HDL

Upon successful completion of the Replication Flow, the data from the table LARGE_DATA in SAP DSP will be transferred to SAP HDLFS in the specified path as PARQUET files by default. This can be verified by navigating to HDLFS using the Database Explorer.

The DSP replication flow will create a new folder within the Root Path of the HDLFS, named after the table. The replication flow creates multiple files (part-*.<extension>) during initial and delta loading. The number and size of these files depends on the source table size and structure as well as change frequency (during delta loading).

entry.title: Use SAP Datasphere as a source for the SAP BW bridge
entry.content:
SAP Datasphere offers a rich set of connectors to access data in SAP and non-SAP systems and to physically load it into SAP Datasphere or virtually access the data in its original location. However, some scenarios might require it to consume data located in SAP Datasphere. One of the components where such a scenario might exist is the SP BW Bridge.

Exposing data from SAP Datasphere is quite straight forward.

​With CDS External Entities there is the possibility to create a RemoteSource with Remotetables. (Communication arrangement, Communication Scenario, Logical External Schema).

Let's take a regular View  in SAP Datasphere which must be exposed for consumption:

​The Datasphere OpenSQL User can be used to create this type of new connection. In addition a Communication Arrangement / -Scenario must be defined.

​All Datasphere Entities, which are enabled for external consumption, can then be used as CDS external entity in the BW Bridge.

​CDS external entities can be used in ABAP code to read the data from Datasphere. For further automation and ETL a custom Process Chain type can be created to include it as a step in a SAP BW/4HANA Process Chain and write the data via the BW Data API to a DataStore or InfoObject:
